name: hcportal_getEventMessages
description: Retrieves all non-deleted event messages for a specific event, returning message content with author information formatted for a chat-like interface. Auth is validated first (consistent with all HC6 SPs). Returns an error if the event is not found. Messages are ordered by creation time descending (newest first).

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: Auth: device ID used to look up the authenticated user and device secret. Validated via HC6.ValidatePortalAuth.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: short-lived cryptographic token, validated via HC6.ValidatePortalAuth with @publicEventId as the context identifier.

parameters.name: publicEventId
parameters.description: Required: public event ID. Validated for NULL after auth. Used to resolve Event.id for message retrieval. Returns 'Event not found' if no matching event exists.

rowsets.name: ErrorEnvelope
rowsets.description: Returned on any validation, auth, or runtime error. Short-circuits on first error. Possible ErrorMessage values: auth errors from ValidatePortalAuth, 'publicEventId is required', 'Event not found'.

rowsets.name: EventMessages
rowsets.description: All non-deleted messages for the event (msg.removed = 0, h.Removed = 0), ordered by createdAt DESC (newest first). Empty rowset if no messages exist. One row per message.

columns.name: type
columns.description: Always the literal string 'text' for all messages

columns.name: roomId
columns.description: The @publicEventId parameter value — same for all rows in a given call

columns.name: createdAt
columns.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds: DATEDIFF_BIG(MILLISECOND, '1970-01-01 00:00:00', EventMessage.createdAt)

columns.name: author
columns.description: JSON object with author info (via JSON_QUERY FOR JSON PATH WITHOUT_ARRAY_WRAPPER): { id: Hasher.PublicHasherId, firstName: Hasher.DisplayName, imageUrl: Hasher.Photo }. NULL if hasher not found (defensive CASE, though INNER JOIN prevents this in practice).

breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change the Unix epoch base date (1970-01-01) used for the createdAt millisecond conversion
Never change the message ordering (createdAt DESC) without frontend coordination
Never change the shape of the author JSON object — callers parse it by field name
